text: Git branching is a powerful feature in version control that helps manage code changes efficiently. Imagine you are working on a new feature. Instead of changing the main codebase directly, you can create a separate line of development called a feature branch. This allows you to experiment and make edits without affecting the stable version. Once your feature is complete, you merge it back into the main branch. In team projects, many people use pull requests to propose these changes. A teammate reviews the code, suggests improvements, and approves it before merging. This workflow keeps the project organized, reduces errors, and makes collaboration smoother.
